title: IISc Administrative Assistant Guess Paper 2
subtitle: Realistic prediction based on 2023 examiner patterns
instructions: Total 80 questions to be attempted in 90 minutes.
Negative marking applies: 0.33333 marks (one-third) are deducted for every incorrect answer.
Figure-based placeholder questions are unscored and carry 0 marks either way.

questions.text: In an election between two candidates, the winner got 55% of the votes and won by 1600 votes. The total number of votes polled was:
questions.explanation: The margin 10% equals 1600, so total is 16000.

questions.text: The selling price of 20 articles equals the cost price of 25 articles. The profit percentage is:
questions.explanation: SP/CP per article = 25/20, a 25% profit.

questions.text: After two successive discounts of 20% and 10%, an article is sold for Rs 720. Its marked price was:
A is twice as efficient as B. Together they finish a work in 12 days. A alone can finish it in:
questions.explanation: Their combined rate is 3 units; A alone takes 36/2 = 18 days.

questions.text: Two pipes can fill a tank in 12 and 16 minutes respectively. When both pipes and a leak at the bottom are open together, the tank fills in 8 minutes. The leak alone can empty the full tank in:
Walking at three-fourths of his usual speed, a man reaches office 20 minutes late. His usual time to reach office is:
questions.explanation: Time becomes 4/3 of usual, so T/3 = 20, T = 60.

questions.text: A boat covers 30 km downstream in 2 hours and 18 km upstream in 3 hours. The speed of the stream is:
questions.explanation: Downstream 15, upstream 6; stream = (15-6)/2 = 4.5.

questions.text: The compound interest on Rs 10000 at 10% per annum for 2 years is:
Rs 1560 is divided among A, B and C in the ratio 1/2 : 1/3 : 1/4. B's share is:
questions.explanation: Ratio becomes 6:4:3; B gets 4/13 of 1560 = 480.

questions.text: Two alloys contain copper and zinc in the ratios 5:3 and 3:1. If equal weights of the two are melted together, the ratio of copper to zinc in the new alloy is:
The least number which when divided by 12, 15 and 20 leaves a remainder of 5 in each case is:
If x + 1/x = 5, enter the value of x^2 + 1/x^2.
A starts a business with Rs 8000 and B with Rs 10000. After 6 months A adds Rs 4000. Out of a year-end profit of Rs 20500, A's share is:
The volume of a hemisphere of radius 21 cm is: (use pi = 22/7)
For what value of k are the lines 3x + ky = 6 and 2x + 4y = 8 parallel?
questions.explanation: 3/2 = k/4 gives k = 6.

questions.text: The ages of A and B are in the ratio 5:7. After 8 years the ratio becomes 7:9. A's present age is:

Sentence 1: The public library was once the heart of every town. Arrange P, Q, R, S. P: Many libraries responded by adding computers and digital archives. Q: With the internet, readers began finding information at home. R: Today the best libraries are community centres as much as book stores. S: Footfall consequently declined through the 2000s.
questions.explanation: Internet arrives, footfall falls, libraries adapt, present state.

questions.text: Sentence 1: Mount Everest attracts climbers from all over the world. Arrange P, Q, R, S. P: Sherpas guide most expeditions through these dangers. Q: The mountain, however, is dangerous, with thin air and sudden storms. R: Even so, hundreds attempt the summit each spring. S: Many climbers pay large sums for a chance at the peak.
questions.explanation: Demand, danger, help from Sherpas, continued attempts.

questions.text: Select the meaning of the idiom: To spill the beans
questions.explanation: It means to disclose secret information.

questions.text: Select the meaning of the idiom: Once in a blue moon
questions.explanation: It means something that happens very rarely.

questions.text: Select the meaning of the idiom: To bury the hatchet
questions.explanation: It means to end a quarrel and become friends.

questions.text: A person who speaks many languages:
questions.explanation: A polyglot knows several languages.

questions.text: The scientific study of birds:
questions.explanation: Ornithology is the study of birds.

questions.text: Select the correctly spelt word:
questions.explanation: Double c and double m: accommodate.

questions.text: Select the incorrectly spelt word:
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is separate.

questions.text: Improve the underlined part: Neither of the two boys WERE present in class.
questions.explanation: 'Neither' takes a singular verb.

questions.text: Improve the underlined part: Rahul is SENIOR THAN me by two years.
questions.explanation: 'Senior' takes 'to', not 'than'.

questions.text: Select the synonym of ABUNDANT:
questions.explanation: Abundant means existing in large quantities.

questions.text: Select the antonym of TRANSPARENT:
questions.explanation: Opaque means not allowing light through.

questions.passage: Solar power has moved from the margins of energy policy to its centre. The cost of photovoltaic panels has fallen by nearly ninety per cent over the past decade, making sunlight the cheapest source of new electricity in many countries. India has used this shift to expand generation capacity rapidly, especially in Rajasthan and Gujarat, where vast solar parks now feed the national grid. Challenges remain, however: the sun does not shine at night, and storing electricity in batteries is still expensive. Until storage becomes cheaper, coal and gas plants will continue to balance the grid.
questions.text: According to the passage, what remains the main obstacle for solar power?

options.text: Lack of land for solar parks

questions.explanation: The passage says battery storage is still costly.

questions.passage: Solar power has moved from the margins of energy policy to its centre. The cost of photovoltaic panels has fallen by nearly ninety per cent over the past decade, making sunlight the cheapest source of new electricity in many countries. India has used this shift to expand generation capacity rapidly, especially in Rajasthan and Gujarat, where vast solar parks now feed the national grid. Challenges remain, however: the sun does not shine at night, and storing electricity in batteries is still expensive. Until storage becomes cheaper, coal and gas plants will continue to balance the grid.
questions.text: The most appropriate title for the passage is:
questions.explanation: The passage traces solar power's growth and challenges.

questions.passage: Solar power has moved from the margins of energy policy to its centre. The cost of photovoltaic panels has fallen by nearly ninety per cent over the past decade, making sunlight the cheapest source of new electricity in many countries. India has used this shift to expand generation capacity rapidly, especially in Rajasthan and Gujarat, where vast solar parks now feed the national grid. Challenges remain, however: the sun does not shine at night, and storing electricity in batteries is still expensive. Until storage becomes cheaper, coal and gas plants will continue to balance the grid.
questions.text: In the passage, 'balance the grid' most nearly means:

options.text: Supply power when solar is unavailable

questions.explanation: Coal and gas fill the gap when the sun is not shining.

questions.text: Find the next term: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?
questions.explanation: Differences 4, 6, 8, 10, then 12.

questions.text: Find the next term: 100, 96, 88, 76, 60, ?
questions.explanation: Differences -4, -8, -12, -16, then -20.

questions.text: Find the next term: 4, 9, 25, 49, 121, ?
questions.explanation: Squares of primes 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, then 13.

questions.text: Find the next term: AZ, BY, CX, ?
questions.explanation: First letters advance, second letters move back.

questions.text: If the letters of the word DELHI are arranged in alphabetical order, how many letters remain in the same position?
questions.explanation: DEHIL vs DELHI: D and E stay put.

questions.text: In a code, TABLE is written as UCEPJ. How is CHAIR written in that code?
questions.explanation: Letters shift by +1, +2, +3, +4, +5.

questions.text: If GO = 22 and SHE = 32, then SOME = ?
questions.explanation: Sum of positions: 19 + 15 + 13 + 5 = 52.

questions.text: In a code language, 'pen pencil book' = 'ka na ta', 'book is new' = 'ta ra sa' and 'pen is blue' = 'ka ra mo'. What is the code for 'book'?
questions.explanation: 'ta' is common to the first two sentences.

questions.text: Six friends A, B, C, D, E, F sit in a row facing north. B is at the extreme left and F at the extreme right. C is third from the left, A is immediately to the right of C, and D is immediately to the left of F. Who sits second from the left?
questions.explanation: Order is B, E, C, A, D, F.

questions.text: Six children sit around a circle facing the centre in the clockwise order A, B, C, D, E, F. Counting to the left (clockwise), who is second to the left of D?
questions.explanation: For centre-facing people, left is clockwise: E, then F.

questions.text: Meena is 15th from the left end of a row and 20th from the right end. How many people are in the row?
If 1st January of a year is a Tuesday, what day of the week is 1st February of the same year?
questions.explanation: 31 days later means +3 days: Friday.

questions.text: Introducing a boy, Meena said, 'He is the son of the only son of my grandfather.' How is the boy related to Meena?
questions.explanation: The only son of her grandfather is her father; his son is her brother.

questions.text: P + Q means P is the brother of Q, P x Q means P is the father of Q, P - Q means P is the sister of Q. Which expression shows that A is the aunt of B?
questions.explanation: A is sister of C and C is father of B, so A is B's aunt.

questions.text: A man walks 4 km east and then 3 km north. The shortest distance from his starting point is:
The value of 5 + 5 x 5 / 5 - 5 is:
questions.explanation: 5 + 5 - 5 = 5 by BODMAS.

questions.text: If '+' means 'x', 'x' means '-', '/' means '+' and '-' means '/', then 8 + 4 x 6 / 3 - 1 = ?
EGIK : FHJL :: MOQS : ?
questions.explanation: Each letter moves one step forward.

questions.text: Find the odd one out: 27, 64, 125, 218
questions.explanation: All others are perfect cubes; 218 is not.

questions.text: Statements: Some cups are plates. All plates are spoons. Conclusions: I. Some spoons are cups. II. All spoons are plates.
questions.explanation: Cups that are plates are also spoons, so I follows.

questions.text: Two positions of the same dice are shown. Find the number on the face opposite to 3.

figure.label: Dice Positions
figure.description: Two views of a numbered dice from which the face opposite 3 must be deduced.

questions.explanation: Unscored placeholder for a visual dice question.

questions.text: A transparent sheet with a pattern is folded along the dotted line. Select the resulting figure.

figure.label: Paper Folding
figure.description: A sheet with a printed pattern and a dotted fold line; the folded appearance must be chosen from four options.

questions.explanation: Unscored placeholder for a visual folding question.

questions.text: How many medals did India win at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games?
questions.explanation: India won 6 medals: 1 silver and 5 bronze.

questions.text: The Nobel Prize in Literature 2024 was awarded to:
questions.explanation: South Korean writer Han Kang won in 2024.

questions.text: ISRO's Aditya-L1 mission is designed to study:
questions.explanation: Aditya-L1 observes the Sun from the L1 point.

questions.text: The repo rate is the rate at which:

options.text: RBI lends short-term funds to banks
Banks lend to each other overnight

questions.explanation: Repo is the RBI's lending rate to commercial banks.

questions.text: The Quit India Movement was launched in:
questions.explanation: It began in August 1942.

questions.text: Who was the first President of the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: W. C. Bonnerjee presided over the 1885 session.

questions.text: The Tehri Dam is built on which river?
questions.explanation: Tehri stands on the Bhagirathi in Uttarakhand.

questions.text: The largest freshwater lake in India is:
questions.explanation: Wular Lake in Jammu and Kashmir is the largest.

questions.text: The Hornbill Festival is celebrated in which state?
questions.explanation: Nagaland hosts the Hornbill Festival every December.

questions.text: A Money Bill is defined under which Article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 110 defines Money Bills.

questions.text: 'An Era of Darkness: The British Empire in India' is written by:
questions.explanation: Shashi Tharoor authored the book.

questions.text: Which country shares the longest land border with India?
questions.explanation: The India-Bangladesh border is about 4096 km.

questions.text: D. Gukesh won the 2024 World Chess Championship by defeating:
questions.explanation: Gukesh beat defending champion Ding Liren.

questions.text: The GST Council is chaired by:
questions.explanation: The Union Finance Minister heads the GST Council.

questions.text: Gaganyaan is India's programme for:
questions.explanation: Gaganyaan aims to send Indian astronauts to orbit.

questions.text: Karpoori Thakur, awarded the Bharat Ratna in 2024, was a former Chief Minister of:
questions.explanation: He served twice as Bihar's Chief Minister.

questions.text: In Excel, what does the formula =MAX(8,12,5)+MIN(8,12,5) return?
In Excel, the formula =IF(15>10,"Yes","No") displays:
questions.explanation: The condition 15>10 is true, so it returns Yes.

questions.text: The universal keyboard shortcut to undo the last action is:
questions.explanation: Ctrl + Z undoes; Ctrl + Y redoes.

questions.text: Which key combination switches between open windows in Windows?
questions.explanation: Alt + Tab cycles through open windows.

questions.text: The BIOS of a computer is typically stored in:
questions.explanation: BIOS firmware resides in ROM/flash memory.

questions.text: The main purpose of cache memory is to:

options.text: Speed up data access between CPU and main memory

questions.explanation: Cache bridges the speed gap between CPU and RAM.

questions.text: Antivirus software is best classified as:
questions.explanation: It is a maintenance/protection utility.

questions.text: Which of the following is application software?
questions.explanation: MS Word is an end-user application.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT an operating system?
questions.explanation: Oracle is a database company/product, not an OS.

questions.text: The characteristic 'diligence' of a computer means:

options.text: It works at very high speed
It can work continuously without fatigue
It can think on its own

questions.explanation: Diligence means tireless, consistent working.
